title: viburnum plicatum f. parvifolium
intro: Viburnum plicatum f. parvifolium, also known as the Koreanspice Viburnum, is a compact, deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its small, white, fragrant flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids, coumarins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Viburnum plicatum f. parvifolium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections, making it a valuable remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
